by_judge.judge: PRADEEP SINGH YERUR IS NOT HOLDING SITTING ON 03.06.2026.

narrative:
On June 3, 2026, the Karnataka High Court recorded an overall hearing rate of 51.1%, resolving 1,192 cases out of 2,333 scheduled across 44 judges. This activity level represents a moderate decrease in disposal efficiency from the previous day, June 2, which maintained a 56.3% hearing rate (1,367 heard of 2,429 scheduled), though it remains close to the 52.3% baseline recorded on June 1.

Individual dockets revealed distinct judicial performance highlights. S. Vishwajith Shetty in Hall 19 managed the highest single workload of the day, successfully hearing 104 out of 186 scheduled matters for a 55.9% conversion rate. Exceptional clearance rates were observed in Hall 10 under Suraj Govindaraj, who disposed of 85.5% of his primary list (65 of 76), and Hall 21 under V. Srishananda, who achieved a 78.2% hearing rate (43 of 55). Conversely, Ashok S. Kinagi in Hall 9 faced a demanding schedule of 151 cases, hearing 48 for a 31.8% conversion rate.

An analysis of hearing stages indicates that Admission matters converted at the highest rate among major stages at 61.9% (260 of 420), followed by Orders at 57.8% (473 of 819). Final Hearings lagged significantly at 26.1% (98 of 376), a sharp decline from the 42.3% conversion achieved on June 2. By case type, Writ Petitions dominated the daily docket with 1,069 scheduled and 517 heard (48.4%), showing a lower clearance level than June 2's 55.2% conversion. Criminal Petitions maintained steady progress at 61.4% (154 of 251), while Criminal Revision Petitions demonstrated high efficiency by converting 81.4% of its 59 scheduled cases.
